summary:
• Iran geopolitical tensions are escalating with stalled nuclear talks and potential U.S. military intervention creating severe supply route risks through the Strait of Hormuz, with U.K. warnings of eight-month price shock duration.

• U.S. LNG export capacity faces structural constraints in replacing lost Qatari supplies, while Shell's $16.4 billion Canadian gas acquisition signals a North American shift in liquefaction investment away from traditional export hubs.

• Alternative energy corridor development around Hormuz through Afghanistan and broader diversification of crude flows (Russian diesel rerouting to Egypt, Iran tankers sailing west) reflects market adaptation to prolonged geopolitical uncertainty in the Persian Gulf.

articles.description: Shell has agreed to acquire ARC Resources, a Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX)-listed energy company operating in the Montney shale basin in Canada, in a cash and stock deal valued at $16.4bn.
